name: Beef Chow Mein
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice beef about 1 inch long, 1/2 inch wide, and as thin as possible.
2. Mix salt, sugar, and soy sauce together and mix well. Add the beef and marinate for at least one hour, longer for more flavor.
3. Wash and slice mushrooms, including stems. Slice bamboo into thin strips; slice scallions into 1-inch pieces; soak, rinse, and drain bean sprouts.
4. In a separate bowl, mix cornstarch and beef stock together.
5. Drain the beef, reserving the marinade. Heat sesame oil and stir-fry beef for 4 to 5 minutes.
6. Add the cornstarch mixture and remaining marinade, bring to a boil stirring constantly, then add vegetables and simmer for 5 minutes longer.
7. Cook noodles in boiling water for 4 to 5 minutes; drain thoroughly.
8. Deep fry as needed, draining on absorbent paper.
9. Beat egg with 1 tablespoon water and pour onto lightly oiled skillet. Make a small thin omelet and slice into thin strips.
10. Place cooked noodles on hot dish, top with beef mixture, and garnish with the strips of omelet.
